findings.page: beach sheet (all beaches) â€” forecast section, mobile + desktop
findings.issue: Raw French sentence rendered on the EN site (and ES too), directly above the 'UNLOCK FORECAST' premium CTA: 'Banc de sargasses detecte a proximite â€” arrivee possible dans 1-3 jours.' A second French variant ('Persistance + vent Open-Meteo. Pas de banc detecte a proximite.') ships on no-bank days. Wrong language in the exact conversion zone destroys credibility for an English-speaking tourist about to pay $9.99.
findings.evidence: Rendered text dumps scripts/tmp-audit-pc/text-mobile-sheet.txt and text-desktop-sheet.txt (both viewports). Source: pipeline emits French in JSON at scripts/lib/forecast.cjs:402 and :408 (plus client-side fallback Sargasses_PROD.jsx:619-621 'Banc detecte pres des plages voisines â€” risque de derive.'); rendered raw with no i18n at Sargasses_PROD.jsx:1991 ({weeklyData.forecastDisclaimer}).
findings.suggestion: Stop rendering pipeline French verbatim. The JSON already carries a machine key (forecastMethod: 'arrival-banks' | 'interpolated' | persistence) â€” map it to localized strings at render with the existing _t()/FC_DAY_MAP pattern (same defensive approach already used for day labels and score labels). Keep FR passthrough for MQ/GP untouched.

findings.issue: Paywall claims '12/12 beaches clean right now Â· satellite 24/7' while the map header the user just saw says '12 BEACHES Â· 10 CLEAN' and Macao + Uvero Alto show orange Moderate markers visible behind the modal. Self-contradiction at the payment moment â€” violates the 'predictions must be convincing before selling' rule.
findings.evidence: text-mobile-paywall.txt: '12/12 beaches clean right now' vs home dump 'BEACHES Â· 10 CLEAN'. Root cause: Sargasses_PROD.jsx:3675 _cleanCount = score>=70 (Macao/Uvero score 75 pass) while map status uses AFAI thresholds (both are 'moderate' in /api/copernicus/sargassum.json).
findings.suggestion: Use the same definition as the map: count status==='clean' from levels (â†’ '10/12 beaches clean right now'). One source of truth for 'clean' across map, list and paywall.

findings.issue: The three feature-preview cards use real beach names with fabricated data that contradicts the live map: card 03 'WEEKEND FORECAST â€” Best for Saturday: Macao Beach â€” Clean all weekend Â· ideal for kids' while Macao is today's WORST beach (Moderate 75, forecast Moderate all 7 days on /sargassum-forecast/) and has kids:false in config. Card 02 invents 'Cabeza de Toro status changed Clean â†’ Moderate' (it is Clean 78 right now). A visitor who just studied the map will catch the lie at the exact moment of purchase.
findings.evidence: Screenshot scripts/tmp-audit-pc/desktop-3-paywall.png + mobile-3-paywall.png. Examples picked by fixed array index, not by data: Sargasses_PROD.jsx:3685-3687 (_exAlert=beaches[4], _exSwitch=beaches[8], _exWeekend=beaches[5]=pc006 Macao) with canned copy at :4152-4177. regions/puntacana.json pc006: status moderate-prone, kids:false.
findings.suggestion: Derive the examples from live weekly data: real best-Saturday beach from weekly forecast, real most-recent status change from history, and only attach 'ideal for kids' if kids:true. Cheap fix, large credibility win â€” the cards become live proof instead of mockups.

findings.issue: Malformed <title>, og:title and twitter:title: 'Beaches Without Sargassum in Punta Cana Today (Live List) (June 10' â€” cut mid-date with a dangling open parenthesis. Looks broken in Google SERP and social shares on the single highest purchase-intent query of the site.
findings.evidence: curl of live page head; same string in og:title/twitter:title. Root cause: smartTrim() in scripts/lib/region-seo-pages.cjs:109-115 cuts at last space â‰¤ max and strips trailing dashes/ampersands but not unbalanced '(â€¦'.
findings.suggestion: Two-line fix in smartTrim: after cutting, strip a trailing unbalanced parenthetical (cut.replace(/\s*\([^)]*$/,'')) â€” or drop '(Live List)' from the template when the date suffix is appended so the title fits 60 chars whole. Rebuild + redeploy regenerates all 5 regions.

findings.page: home/map â€” first screen, mobile + desktop
findings.issue: Base map tiles intermittently fail to load, leaving large dark holes across the first screen (the product IS this map); page reached networkidle only after ~32s in both viewports. Single tile provider with zero error handling or fallback â€” when CARTO hiccups, a first-time visitor from Google sees a broken map and bounces before ever meeting the paywall.
findings.evidence: Screenshots scripts/tmp-audit-pc/mobile-1-home.png and desktop-1-home.png (checkerboard of missing tiles after 9s wait, reproduced across 3 separate sessions); console: 4x 'net::ERR_CONNECTION_RESET'; direct curl to a.basemaps.cartocdn.com: 3 consecutive connection failures (000, 21-25s timeout) then 200s at 1.6-3.5s/tile. Tile layer: src/MapView.jsx:153, no 'tileerror' handler. Caveat: partially network-dependent â€” verify from a second network, but resilience is missing regardless.
findings.suggestion: Add a tileerror handler that retries once then swaps to a fallback provider (e.g. OSM standard or a second CARTO endpoint), set keepBuffer, and add <link rel=preconnect> for basemaps.cartocdn.com in the HTML head. Also consider showing a flat ocean-colored background under tiles so failures degrade gracefully instead of to near-black.

findings.issue: 8 of 12 beaches show the identical score 83/100 'GREAT' with byte-identical factor breakdowns, and near-identical 7-day forecasts; AFAI spans only 0.12-0.15 across all 12. The list reads 83-83-83-83 â€” the per-beach differentiation that the site itself sells ('a mat that buries Macao can miss Cap Cana entirely' on /sargassum-forecast/) is invisible, weakening the premium argument ('which beach should I pick?' answer looks trivial).
findings.evidence: /api/copernicus/sargassum.json: pc001-pc004, pc008-pc011 all score 83 with identical breakdown {sargassum:30,wave:17,wind:13,sst:10,cloud:5,uv:5,tide:3}; screenshots mobile-4-list.png/desktop-4-list.png.
findings.suggestion: Audit the PC per-beach AFAI sampling boxes (likely overlapping for the Bavaro cluster) and the wave/wind sampling resolution for the region â€” possibly related to the 1D-weighting fix of 2026-06-09. Even genuine 1-2 point spreads between neighbors restore the 'every beach differs' story. Off-season, also surface non-sargassum differentiators (swell, crowds) more aggressively in the breakdown.

findings.issue: Soft-404: every nonexistent URL (tested /zzz-not-a-page/) returns HTTP 200 with the home SPA shell. With 56 sitemap pages plus 5 cross-linked sister domains, this invites duplicate-content and soft-404 flags in GSC and wastes crawl budget â€” relevant given the documented MQ/GP indexation gap.
findings.suggestion: At hosting level, restrict the SPA rewrite to known prefixes (/, /es, /beaches/, /resorts/, hub slugs) and return real 404 + minimal 404.html elsewhere; alternatively render a noindex meta into the fallback shell for unknown paths via JS as a stopgap.

findings.issue: Spanish is promised in config (secondaryLangs, differentiator 'sargazo punta cana hoy') and the SPA has a complete ES dictionary + working ES toggle, but the SEO layer is EN-only: zero /es/ pages in the sitemap, no hreflang es anywhere, and /es/ serves html lang="en". The DR is a Spanish-speaking country and Latino travel queries go entirely uncaptured â€” Google cannot discover the ES experience at all.
findings.evidence: sitemap.xml has 56 EN URLs, 0 ES; home head: only hreflang en + x-default; curl /es/ â†’ same EN shell (lang="en"). region-seo-pages.cjs already contains the full ES template strings (T.es, playas/, hoteles/ dirs) â€” wired but unused for this region.
findings.suggestion: Generate the ES hub + beach pages for puntacana via the existing T.es path in region-seo-pages.cjs (the code is already there), add reciprocal hreflang en/es/x-default to both versions, and set lang=es on ES documents. Low effort, opens the second language market the config already commits to.

findings.issue: Title truncation pattern leaves dangling fragments in SERP: 'Juanillo Beach (Cap Cana) Sargassum Today â€” Live Status & 7-Day' and 'Playa Blanca (Cap Cana) Sargassum Today â€” Live Status & 7-Day' (missing 'Forecast'). Less broken than the date case but the same root cause, and these are commercial landing pages.
findings.evidence: Live <title> tags via curl; smartTrim max in scripts/lib/region-seo-pages.cjs against template 'X Sargassum Today â€” Live Status & 7-Day Forecast'.
findings.suggestion: When the full template exceeds the title budget, fall back to a shorter template ('X Sargassum Today â€” Live Status & Forecast', then 'X Sargassum Today & Forecast') instead of character-trimming a fixed string. Also drop the parenthetical '(Cap Cana)' from titles where space is tight â€” it survives in the H1 and description.

findings.page: beach sheet â€” email capture placement
findings.issue: The free email-capture card ('FREE â€” Where's the cleanest beach today? We tell you every morning. Free.') sits ABOVE the tomorrow-forecast teaser and the premium forecast lock. On mobile the sheet opens to photo + score + email box, with the forecast (the premium hook) below the fold â€” a competing free CTA intercepts attention before the paid value proposition is even seen.
findings.evidence: Screenshot mobile-2-sheet.png (email box is the last visible element at sheet open; TOMORROW FORECAST and the lock require scroll); order in BeachSheet, Sargasses_PROD.jsx ~1848+.
findings.suggestion: A/B the order: forecast teaser + lock first, email capture below (or after first scroll). The email list is a real asset (drip â†’ premium), but at the moment of maximum intent ('what about tomorrow?') the premium teaser should win the first viewport.

findings.issue: Contradictory status signals for the same beach: Macao and Uvero Alto display green-tinted '75/100 GOOD' in the list while their map markers are orange and their API status is 'moderate' (and the header count '10 CLEAN' excludes them). A tourist comparing list and map gets three different verdicts for one beach.
findings.evidence: Screenshots mobile-4-list.png (green 75 GOOD, orange photo dot) vs mobile-1-home.png (orange 75 badges); /api/copernicus/sargassum.json status 'moderate' for pc006/pc007.
findings.suggestion: Make the score chip inherit the status color (orange for moderate) and reconsider the label band: 75 = 'GOOD' while status = Moderate is the jarring case â€” either lower the GOOD threshold band or display 'FAIR' when sargassum status is moderate, so color + word + number tell one story.

findings.page: premium paywall modal â€” plan default
findings.issue: Annual ($79/yr, -33%) is preselected and the main CTA subtext reads 'Then $79/yr Â· cancel in 1 click'. The PC audience is overwhelmingly 1-2 week vacationers; an annual-first ask can feel oversized for a one-trip need and add hesitation at the decision point.
findings.evidence: Screenshots mobile-3-paywall.png / desktop-3-paywall.png: Annual card highlighted, CTA subtext 'Then $79/yr'.
findings.suggestion: A/B default plan for USD vacation regions: monthly preselected (matches the trip mental model, $9.99 reads as trivial vs a $5k vacation), annual as visible upsell. Measure with the existing sg_premium_modal_cta plan tracking.

findings.issue: The 'locked' days 2-7 forecast values are present in the DOM in clear text (15% TMRW, 15% FRI, 14% SAT readable via copy/inspect) â€” the lock is blur-only. Negligible for tourists, but the premium data is scrapeable and selectable text can leak through the blur on some renderers.
findings.evidence: text-mobile-sheet.txt contains all locked-day percentages and statuses while UI shows ðŸ”’ UNLOCK FORECAST.
findings.suggestion: Render placeholder values (e.g. 'Â·Â·%') for locked days for non-premium users and swap in real values only when isPremium â€” same visual, no leak.

findings.issue: No brand wordmark is visible anywhere on the home map screen (mobile header: LIVE chip, moon, ES; desktop adds only 'Caribbean View'). A first-time SEO visitor is later asked for $9.99/mo by a product whose name they have never seen on screen â€” weak trust anchor, and zero brand recall for the return visit ('that map siteâ€¦').
findings.evidence: Screenshots mobile-1-home.png / desktop-1-home.png; the 'SARGASSUM PUNTA CANA' wordmark (Sargasses_PROD.jsx:2493) does not appear on the map view.
findings.suggestion: Add a small wordmark to the existing header chrome (e.g. left of the LIVE chip, 11px caps, same pill style â€” no clutter). It also anchors the paywall: the modal can then reference the named product.

findings.issue: Several list thumbnails crop to mostly sky/clouds (Bavaro, Playa Los Corales, Arena Gorda) rather than sand/water; on desktop the thumbnails compress into thin slivers showing almost no beach. The photos are doing none of their persuasion work.
findings.suggestion: Set object-position to favor the lower 2/3 of each photo (sand/water) or curate crop offsets in beaches-images.json; give desktop list rows a slightly taller thumbnail.

findings.page: global â€” push primer banner (desktop observed)
findings.issue: First-visit banner says 'Get notified when your favorite beaches change.' to a visitor who has zero favorites yet â€” the copy references state that does not exist. The primer pattern itself is fine (data-justified: 6% opt-in measured, 7-day cooldown, dismissible).
findings.evidence: desktop-2-sheet.png top banner; copy at Sargasses_PROD.jsx:2618; rationale comment :2594-2601.
findings.suggestion: Either gate the primer on â‰¥1 favorite (then the copy is true and the ask is contextual) or use favorites-neutral copy for first-timers: 'Get notified when Punta Cana beaches change.'
